item.title: Can We Ask Our Son to Go on Weight-Loss Drugs in Exchange for a House?
item.description: My husband wants to condition the gift on our son’s getting on a GLP-1 program. I feel that a gift is a gift and you should not extort a grown man.

item.title: How Russia Weaponized the Cold Ukrainian Winter
item.description: Inside one Kyiv neighborhood as it braved the harshest conditions since World War II.

item.title: What One Month of Intense Red-Light Therapy Did to My Mind
item.description: Celebrities are obsessed. People swear it gives them thicker hair and perfect skin. But what if the biggest effect of red light is stranger than that?

item.title: My Friend Wrote a Book About Her Trauma. Do I Have to Read It?
item.description: She told me that anyone who won’t read her book is not her friend and that I am contributing to the greater problem in the world.

item.title: Worried About A.I. Taking Your Job? That’s Not Very ‘Agentic’ of You.
item.description: Today’s spin on the idea of personal agency is convenient for tech C.E.O.s, who boast that their models work just fine without us.

item.title: Is There a Proper Way to Eat Chip Dust?
item.description: A ruling on the consumption of snack dregs.

item.title: My Fiancé’s Mother Is Out of Control. Does She Have to Come to the Wedding?
item.description: I can’t put my family through what she put us through on Thanksgiving.

item.title: My Brother Keeps Falling for Online Romance Scams. Should I Bail Him Out Again?
item.description: When I hesitate to give him money, he guilts me.

item.title: Vegetative Patients May Be More Aware Than We Knew
item.description: New research is upending what we thought about the consciousness of patients, leaving families with agonizing choices.

item.title: How Did ‘Gatekeeping’ Become a Cardinal Sin?
item.description: We decided that filtering information was malicious. That didn’t mean we stopped doing it.

item.title: Two Diaries of War, From Opposing Sides of Iran’s Political Divide
item.description: One supports the regime, the other desires change. Each offers a personal account of the terrifying sights and frayed emotions of a city under attack.

item.title: Forget the A.I. Apocalypse. Memes Have Already Nuked Our Culture.
item.description: From our jokes and slang to the White House’s policy messaging, internet “brain rot” has escaped our phones to take over … well, everything.

item.title: Why Am I Watching People Get Their Medical Results?
item.description: What was once discussed with a doctor is now frequently encountered first as decontextualized data on a screen.

item.title: The Hair-Loss Drug Rewriting the Rules of Masculinity
item.description: A pill to cure baldness is changing the way men age — and how they see themselves.
